steps.title: Find the slope of line r from the given points
steps.content:
Line $r$ passes through $(2, 16)$ and $(7, 17)$. Using the slope formula:
$$m_r = \frac{17-16}{7-2} = \frac{1}{5}$$

steps.title: Apply the perpendicular lines property
steps.content:
When two lines are perpendicular, their slopes are negative reciprocals. Since $m_r = \frac{1}{5}$:
$$m_s = -\frac{1}{\frac{1}{5}} = -5$$

steps.title: Write the equation of line s using the given point
steps.content:
Line $s$ passes through $(1, 2)$ with slope $-5$. Using point-slope form:
$$y - 2 = -5(x - 1)$$
$$y - 2 = -5x + 5$$
$$y = -5x + 7$$
$$5x + y = 7$$

steps.title: Convert to the form ax + 7y = c
steps.content:
Multiply both sides of $5x + y = 7$ by 7 to match the required form:
$$35x + 7y = 49$$
Therefore, $c = 49$.
